title: M-PPG: A Nonintrusive Authentication for Wearable Devices via PPG Sensors With Motor Excitations

method.input_representation: 35 selected features comprising temporal statistics, frequency-domain (FFT), and time-frequency-domain (DWT) features from heartbeat-related, motor-related, and overall PPG signals
method.training_strategy: Binary classifier trained for each legitimate user

evaluation.context: Pass rate of attackers with a well-designed authentication mechanism
Derived from the legitimate user pass rate of 99.9%

evaluation_protocol: Leave-one-user-out style where each participant acts as the legitimate user and the others act as attackers

reported_limitations: Performance decline when heart rate exceeds 100 bpm

key_contribution: Introduced motor excitations as a stimulus to provide stable high-frequency signals in PPG for better authentication
Proposed a random challenge scheme and adaptive update mechanism for long-term security
main_strength: Effective noise reduction for indoor lighting strobe interference
Robustness against motion artifacts compared to passive PPG
main_weakness: Experimental security analysis is theoretical rather than empirical (no active spoofing tests performed)
insights.notes_for_review: The paper uses 'Pass Rate' which maps to FAR for attackers and (1-FRR) for legitimate users.
